rationale_for_baseline_tier: A first-degree sunburn without blistering or systemic heat illness is self-managed with analgesia, cooling, emollient and hydration (T0). Blistering/extensive burn or heat exhaustion/stroke routes to ED (T4/T5).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Large fluid-filled blisters, very extensive burn, or signs of infection (spreading redness, pus, fever)
escalation_edges.communication_template: If large blisters form, the burn is very extensive, or it becomes infected (spreading redness, pus, fever) — go to the Emergency Department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe headache with confusion, repeated vomiting, dizziness/faintness, a very high temperature, or not passing urine
escalation_edges.rationale: Heat stroke is life-threatening; significant dehydration needs assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a severe headache with confusion, keep vomiting, feel very faint, have a very high temperature, or stop passing urine — call 000 or go to ED.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear heat-illness and blistering red flags for self-monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Take anti-inflammatory pain relief and paracetamol, cool the skin with damp compresses and moisturiser, drink extra fluids, and keep out of the sun. It peaks at about a day then settles and peels.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care; ED for blistering/extensive burn or heat-illness features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000/ED for heat-stroke features; ED for large blisters.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: First-degree solar erythema; deeper-burn and heat-illness screens negative; symptomatic care + hydration + sun avoidance.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Concurrent heat stroke is missed and the patient collapses.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No depth assessment, missing an evolving blistering burn.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a simple first-degree sunburn with a negative screen — unnecessary.
